_description: Shared model registry for all AIXIA-workspace projects. Projects resolve model family → current_version at runtime from this file. Maintained by the bi-weekly model-release crawler (scheduled trigger). See ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md §9.5 for policy.
_bootstrap_note: This is the bootstrap state from 2026-04-20. Entries marked bootstrap_unverified: true are seeded from CLAUDE Code context and should be verified on the first crawler run. Pricing drifts; registry values are authoritative — always re-read at call-time.

claude_opus.notes: claude-opus-4-7 released April 16, 2026 (date corrected from bootstrap estimate of 2026-01-01). Same price as Opus 4.6 ($5/$25). Key improvements: SWE-bench Verified 80.8% → 87.6%, SWE-bench Pro 53.4% → 64.3%, CursorBench 58% → 70%. Vision resolution 3.3× increase (1,568px → 2,576px long edge). New tokenizer — same input maps to 1.0–1.35× more tokens than Opus 4.6; audit token budgets. Claude Mythos (Project Glasswing, 50-org restricted) confirmed but not yet general API-available.

claude_sonnet.notes: Pricing and version verified May 2026. No change from last crawl.

claude_haiku.notes: Pricing and version verified May 2026. No change from last crawl.

deepseek_general.notes: MAJOR UPDATE: deepseek-v3.2 → deepseek-v4-flash (released April 24, 2026). Context window 128K → 1M tokens. Pricing dropped: $0.28/$0.42 → $0.14/$0.28 (~50% input cut). Architecture: 284B total / 13B active params (MoE). Supports Thinking and Non-Thinking dual mode. Full 1M context included at standard rate (no surcharge). Compatible with both OpenAI ChatCompletions and Anthropic API formats. For higher-performance V4 tasks see deepseek_v4_pro family entry. Cache hits drop to 1/10 of standard rate. Chinese-server jurisdiction — disclose in ToS. Router fallback: deepseek → claude_haiku → claude_sonnet.

deepseek_reasoning.notes: Pricing confirmed $0.55/$2.19 (cache hits: $0.14/M input). DeepSeek-R2 not yet released as of May 2026. R1 remains ~96% cheaper than OpenAI o1 for comparable tasks. Note: deepseek-v4-pro (see deepseek_v4_pro entry) also supports Thinking mode at 1M context if R1's 64K context is too small for a task.

deepseek_v4_pro.notes: NEW ENTRY (May 4, 2026). DeepSeek-V4-Pro: 1.6T total / 49B active params (large MoE), 1M context, Thinking+Non-Thinking dual mode. Standard pricing $1.74/$3.48; 75% promotional discount active through 2026-05-31 (effective $0.435/$0.87 during promo). 🚨 Promo expiry June 1 — standard rate $1.74/$3.48 resumes; audit any project budget using this model at promo rate. Compatible with OpenAI ChatCompletions and Anthropic API formats. Use this tier for tasks needing V4-scale performance with reasoning, where deepseek_general (V4 Flash) isn't sufficient and deepseek_reasoning (R1) hits context limits.

gemini_pro_multimodal.notes: Version and pricing verified May 2026; no change from last crawl. Tiered pricing: $2/$12 up to 200K tokens; $4/$18 above 200K. Scored 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2. Handles YouTube URL ingest via file_data.file_uri. New this cycle: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS Preview (text-to-speech capability, not a general LLM) and Veo 3.1 Lite Preview (cost-efficient video generation) were released — not tracked as separate registry families but note expansion of Google multimodal surface area.

gemini_flash.notes: Version and pricing verified May 2026; no change from last crawl. Budget alternative: gemini-3.1-flash-lite at $0.25/$1.50 for cheapest bulk tasks. Also note: Gemma 4 31B (Apache 2.0, open-weight, #3 on Arena AI) available as local/self-hosted inference option.

openai_gpt.notes: GPT-5.4 pricing and version verified May 2026; no change from last crawl. Standard $2.50/$15 covers up to 272K context; above 272K: 2× input, 1.5× output. Also: gpt-5.4-mini ($0.75/$4.50, 400K, Mar 17 2026); gpt-5.4-pro ($30/$180). Observation: a GPT-5.5 model page appeared in OpenAI API docs during this crawl — unconfirmed release; being conservative, version not bumped. Monitor next cycle. Not currently used by AIXIA projects.

openai_reasoning.notes: Verified May 2026; no change from last crawl. o4-mini outperforms o3-mini at ~50% lower cost. o3 also available ($2.00/M input, 200K context). IMPORTANT: reasoning tokens billed at output rate — effective cost 5–20× listed rate depending on task complexity. Not currently used by AIXIA projects.

mistral_large.notes: Pricing and version verified May 2026; no change from last crawl. Mistral Large 3 2512 released December 1 2025; 262K context. EU-hosted option for data-residency-sensitive tiers. See also mistral_small and new mistral_medium entries for cheaper/mid-tier EU options.

mistral_small.notes: Pricing and version verified May 2026; no change from last crawl. Mistral Small 4 (mistral-small-2603): MoE 119B total / 6B active params, 256K context, native multimodal (text + image). Announced NVIDIA GTC March 17 2026. Cheapest EU-residency multimodal option in registry at $0.15/$0.60.

mistral_medium.notes: NEW ENTRY (May 4, 2026). Mistral Medium 3.5 released April 29, 2026. Dense 128B open-weight model that unifies instruction-following, reasoning, and coding into a single set of weights (previously these were separate Magistral/Pixtral/Devstral products). 256K context. EU data residency available. API model ID follows Mistral naming convention (2604 = April 2026). Fills the gap between mistral_small ($0.15/$0.60 MoE) and mistral_large ($0.50/$1.50) — notably, output cost ($7.50) is 5× mistral_large, so only worth it for tasks requiring the unified reasoning+coding capability. Relevant for Echoform EU tiers needing stronger reasoning than Small can provide.

llama.notes: Verified May 2026; no change from last crawl. Llama 4 Maverick: 17B active params, 128 experts (400B total), 1M context, natively multimodal MoE. API pricing reflects third-party providers. Also: Llama 4 Scout (same price, 10M context, 16 experts, 109B total) for extreme-context self-hosted tasks; Llama 4 Behemoth (288B active, 16 experts) announced but not yet generally available. Rumored next-gen model codename 'Avocado' reported but unconfirmed. Both Scout and Maverick Apache 2.0 open-weight.

grok.notes: UPDATED: grok-4.20-0309-v2 → grok-4.3 (full API rollout completed April 30, 2026). Pricing: $2.00/$6.00 → $1.25/$2.50 (38% input cut, 58% output cut — net positive). 🚨 Context window REDUCED: 2M → 1M tokens — any AIXIA project relying on Grok for >1M-token calls must migrate to gemini_pro_multimodal (2M context) or gemini_flash (1M). Reasoning is always active and cannot be disabled. First xAI API model with native video frame input (not transcribe-then-feed). Tiered pricing: requests exceeding 200K total tokens billed at higher rate. Grok 4 Heavy (256K context, parallel test-time compute, highest benchmarks) only available via SuperGrok Heavy consumer tier — NOT via API.

routing_defaults.description: Canonical default routing for common task categories. Projects override as needed in their local CLAUDE.md.

privacy_mode_routing.description: When a project/tier enables Privacy Mode, all calls route through Claude only (no DeepSeek, no third-party-jurisdiction routes). Studio-tier Echoform users default to Privacy Mode.
